title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) with our easy recipe! Learn to marinate and grill these flavorful ribs for a perfect Korean dinner.
longTailKeywords: how to make Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs
authentic Korean BBQ short ribs recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: Korean BBQ short ribs marinade recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?

seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) with sesame seeds and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, savory, and slightly sweet flavors of homemade Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi).

description: Galbi is a beloved Korean BBQ dish featuring tender, marinated beef short ribs. These flanken-cut ribs are steeped in a savory-sweet soy sauce-based marinade, infused with garlic, ginger, and fruit, then grilled to perfection. The result is an incredibly flavorful and succulent experience, making it a centerpiece for any Korean feast.

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the flanken-cut beef short ribs under cold running water to remove any bone fragments or impurities. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. This step ensures a clean surface for marinade absorption and better browning.

instructions.title: Make the Galbi Marinade
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, mirin, sesame oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, grated Asian pear, grated onion, and black pepper. Whisk all ingredients vigorously until the brown sugar is completely dissolved and the marinade is well combined.

instructions.title: Marinate the Ribs
instructions.text: Place the dried short ribs into the bowl with the prepared marinade. Ensure that each rib is fully submerged and coated. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or transfer the ribs and marinade into a large zip-top bag. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight (12-24 hours) for maximum flavor penetration and tenderness.

instructions.title: Preheat Grill and Cook
instructions.text: Preheat your grill (gas or charcoal) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking. Remove the marinated ribs from the refrigerator and let any excess marinade drip off. Place the ribs on the hot grill and cook for 3-5 minutes per side, or until they have beautiful char marks and are cooked to your desired doneness. Adjust heat as necessary to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Rest the Cooked Ribs
instructions.text: Once the short ribs are perfectly grilled, transfer them to a clean cutting board or a platter. Tent them loosely with aluminum foil and let them rest for at least 5 minutes. This crucial step allows the meat juices to redistribute throughout the ribs, resulting in a more tender and moist final product.

instructions.title: Slice, Garnish, and Serve
instructions.text: After resting, slice the Galbi between the bones into individual pieces, or into smaller bite-sized portions if preferred. Arrange the sliced ribs on a serving platter. Garnish generously with freshly chopped green onions and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately with steamed rice and an assortment of traditional Korean banchan (side dishes).

tips: Always use fresh Asian pear and onion in the marinade; their enzymes tenderize the meat naturally and add a subtle sweetness.
For the best flavor, marinate the short ribs for at least 12 hours, or even up to 24 hours. The longer the marination, the deeper the taste.
Do not overcrowd the grill. Cook the ribs in batches if necessary to ensure even cooking and proper caramelization without steaming the meat.
article: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs is a beloved Korean Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
